agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement notification preferences feature. Bird produced two submissions — v2 supersedes v1 after legal review. The key change: transactional email opt-out (v1's AC2) was removed entirely in v2. Users may opt out of marketing emails only. Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances per legal requirement. MJ's architecture aligns with Bird's v2.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances (legal requirement)","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to set transactional_emails_enabled to false must be rejected with 403 and error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED"},{"rule":"Marketing emails can be freely toggled by the user","testable_assertion":"A user can set marketing_emails_enabled to true or false and the change persists"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences endpoint","implementation_note":"Partial update semantics. Accepts JSON body with preference fields to update."},{"decision":"marketing_emails_enabled stored as boolean field","implementation_note":"Defaults to true for new users (opt-out model, not opt-in)."},{"decision":"Server-side validation rejects transactional_emails_enabled = false","implementation_note":"Return 403 with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. Do NOT silently ignore the field — fail explicitly."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"User can opt out of marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = true","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as false"},{"criterion":"User can opt back in to marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = false","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as true"},{"criterion":"Transactional email opt-out is prohibited","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"403 Forbidden with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. No preferences are modified."}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Transactional emails","technical_term":"transactional_emails_enabled","definition":"System-mandated emails such as password resets and security alerts. Cannot be disabled per legal requirement."},{"domain_term":"Marketing emails","technical_term":"marketing_emails_enabled","definition":"Promotional and non-essential communications. User may freely opt out."}],"contradictions_resolved":[{"conflict":"Bird v1 included AC2 allowing transactional email opt-out, while the v1 domain rule said transactional emails 'cannot be fully disabled.' These were in tension.","resolution":"Bird v2 resolved this: AC2 was removed entirely after legal review. Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances. v2 supersedes v1."}],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are clear and legally grounded","Bird v2 and MJ are fully aligned on behavior","403 response for transactional opt-out attempt is specified by both Bird and MJ"],"low_confidence_areas":["Default value for marketing_emails_enabled not explicitly stated by Bird — assumed true (opt-out model)","Whether the PATCH should reject the entire request or only the transactional field when a mixed payload is sent (e.g. both marketing and transactional changes) — brief assumes atomic rejection"],"assumptions":["Bird v2 is authoritative and fully supersedes v1","New users default to marketing_emails_enabled = true","A PATCH containing transactional_emails_enabled = false should fail the entire request, not partially apply other fields"]}}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the inputs from Bird and MJ for the "notification preferences" feature.

Bird has two submissions - v1 and v2. v2 is the later one and supersedes v1. Key change: AC2 (transactional email opt-out) was removed in v2 after legal review.

MJ's submission aligns with Bird's v2 - the API validates that transactional_emails_enabled cannot be set to false, returning 403.

No contradictions - Bird v2 and MJ are aligned. Let me produce the handoff brief.
